title: Boat Rental in Lakshadweep: Essential Tips for 2026
html:
Exploring the turquoise atolls of Lakshadweep by boat is the most rewarding way to see this remote archipelago. Whether you depart from Kavaratti or Agatti, expect to pay from €150 for a half-day trip. BoatHire24 compares verified operators so you can find the perfect vessel for your needs.

Where to set off in Lakshadweep

Most boat rentals begin at the main islands of Kavaratti, Agatti, and Minicoy. These have small harbours and basic jetties that handle day charters and liveaboard departures.

Kavaratti harbour is the busiest, offering sheltered berths for vessels up to 20 metres. Agatti Island has an airstrip and a lagoon ideal for speedboat launches. Minicoy Island, with its lighthouse and colonial buildings, is a popular overnight stop. Bangaram and Thinnakara atolls are uninhabited but frequently visited on multi-day itineraries.

Which boat to rent

  1. Speedboat (Olympic 490): Best for short island hops and snorkelling trips; carries 6 guests; from €180 for a half-day.
  2. Motor yacht (Mefasa 90): Ideal for luxury day charters with a crew; sleeps 10; from €1,200 per day. For detailed specifications, see the Mefasa 90 listing on BoatHire24.
  3. Catamaran (Lagoon 52): Stable and spacious for longer cruises; accommodates 12; from €800 per day.
  4. Traditional dhoni: Great for cultural experiences and lagoon tours; up to 8 guests; from €100 for a half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Lakshadweep?

Prices vary by boat type and duration, starting from around €100 for a shared tour.

  • Shared group tour (dhoni): €50, €80 per person for a 4-hour trip.
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 6 guests): €150, €300.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): €600, €1,000.
  • Crewed yacht (overnight, 8 guests): €2,000, €3,500 per night.

These rates are typical for 2026 and reflect demand during high season. Always confirm inclusions like fuel and crew tips when you compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Lakshadweep?

For bareboat rental you need an ICC or equivalent; skippered charters require no licence from you.

Indian regulations mandate a valid International Certificate of Competence for self-drive hire in Lakshadweep waters. Most visitors opt for a skippered charter, where the operator provides a local captain who knows the reefs and currents.

During December to April, the north-east monsoon keeps the sea calm around the atolls, making this the prime window for island-hopping.

When is the best season for boat rental?

High season runs from December to April, when the north-east monsoon brings calm seas and clear skies.

From May to November, the south-west monsoon brings rough conditions and many operators suspend trips. In 2026, book well in advance for December and January charters, as availability becomes tight.

At a glance

  • Price range: €100, €3,500 per trip
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Typical trip length: half-day or full-day
  • Maximum guests: 6, 12 depending on boat
  • Currency: EUR (most operators quote in euros for international clients)

Questions about boat rental in Lakshadweep

Can I book a boat directly from Kavaratti airport?

Yes, several operators offer transfers direct from Kavaratti airstrip to the harbour, where your charter departs.

Are there overnight hire options between the islands?

Multi-day liveaboard trips are available, typically for groups of 6, 10 guests, with a crew and meals included.

What happens if the weather turns bad during my trip?

Operators monitor forecasts closely and will postpone or reroute to protect guests and vessels; most offer full refunds if cancelled before departure.

Is it safe to swim and snorkel from a rented boat?

Yes, provided you use a life jacket and follow the crew’s safety briefing; many boats provide snorkel gear free of charge.

Whether you are a seasoned sailor or a first-time renter, using a platform like BoatHire24 makes it simple to compare options and secure a trusted charter. Boat owners in Lakshadweep can also list your boat on the marketplace and reach a global audience.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Lakshadweep and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
